mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, provides a favorable environment for equity valuations by reducing uncertainty and supporting consumer purchasing power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL remains below 2.5% YoY but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes or coincides with economic downturns, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, which is highly conducive to corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows strong growth while FEDFUNDS is low, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.05%) in more than 40% of triggered periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS, higher borrowing costs for corporations and consumers tend to depress economic activity and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises significantly during a tightening cycle, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.2%) or the Rank IC is below 0.05 during such periods over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong underlying economic demand and consumer confidence, which typically translates into broader economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST consistently exceeds 1200k units but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, indicating a weakening economy that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of such occurrences over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently grows by more than 150k for three months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment (UNRATE) and strong retail sales (RSAFS) signifies a robust consumer base with both the ability and willingness to spend, driving corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE is below 4.0% and RSAFS shows monthly growth above 0.3%, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 35% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.
